panels.title: Board MC over time
panels.description: One line per selected probe. Reliable window ~7–25% MC; below ~7% the reading floors and very wet wood clamps at 28%.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "woodstack" and r.kind == "wood"
  and r.stack =~ /${stack:regex}/
  and r.thickness =~ /${thickness:regex}/
  and r.level =~ /${level:regex}/
  and r.board =~ /${board:regex}/
  and r.spot =~ /${spot:regex}/
  and r.depth =~ /${depth:regex}/ and r._field == "mc")
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "stack", "level", "thickness", "board", "spot", "depth"])
  |> group(columns: ["stack", "level", "thickness", "board", "spot", "depth"])

panels.title: Latest MC per probe
panels.description: One row per selected probe. The sparkline shows recent history with the most recent MC value inline; colored by wetness.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "woodstack" and r.kind == "wood"
  and r.stack =~ /${stack:regex}/
  and r.thickness =~ /${thickness:regex}/
  and r.level =~ /${level:regex}/
  and r.board =~ /${board:regex}/
  and r.spot =~ /${spot:regex}/
  and r.depth =~ /${depth:regex}/ and r._field == "mc")
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "stack", "level", "thickness", "board", "spot", "depth"])
  |> group(columns: ["stack", "level", "thickness", "board", "spot", "depth"])

panels.title: Raw pin-pair resistance (log)
panels.description: The measured resistance behind each MC value. Log scale. Above ~1 MΩ the ESP32 ADC noise floor makes MC unreliable (reads too dry).

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "woodstack" and r.kind == "wood"
  and r.stack =~ /${stack:regex}/
  and r.thickness =~ /${thickness:regex}/
  and r.level =~ /${level:regex}/
  and r.board =~ /${board:regex}/
  and r.spot =~ /${spot:regex}/
  and r.depth =~ /${depth:regex}/ and r._field == "resistance")
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "stack", "level", "thickness", "board", "spot", "depth"])
  |> group(columns: ["stack", "level", "thickness", "board", "spot", "depth"])

panels.title: Shell–core spread (shallow − deep)
panels.description: Per board where both depths exist: shallow MC minus deep MC. Keep it under ~4–5 points to avoid case-hardening / surface checking. Needs both a shallow and a deep probe selected on the same board.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "woodstack" and r.kind == "wood"
  and r.stack =~ /${stack:regex}/
  and r.thickness =~ /${thickness:regex}/
  and r.level =~ /${level:regex}/
  and r.board =~ /${board:regex}/
  and r.spot =~ /${spot:regex}/
  and r.depth =~ /${depth:regex}/ and r._field == "mc")
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time", "stack", "level", "thickness", "board", "spot"], columnKey: ["depth"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => exists r.shallow and exists r.deep)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, stack: r.stack, level: r.level, thickness: r.thickness, board: r.board, spot: r.spot, _value: r.shallow - r.deep}))
  |> group(columns: ["stack", "level", "thickness", "board", "spot"])

list.name: stack
list.label: Stack

query.query:
import "influxdata/influxdb/schema"
schema.tagValues(bucket: "${bucket}", tag: "stack", predicate: (r) => r._measurement == "woodstack" and r.kind == "wood", start: -180d)

list.definition:
import "influxdata/influxdb/schema"
schema.tagValues(bucket: "${bucket}", tag: "stack", predicate: (r) => r._measurement == "woodstack" and r.kind == "wood", start: -180d)
